key_insight: Glassy polymers yield and neck because stretching increases free volume, which “unfreezes” shear mobility and transitions the material from frozen to plastic flow.
engineering_relevance: The model gives a compact constitutive law that can predict yielding stress and neck-initiation conditions versus strain rate and temperature for polymer design and process planning.
method: Start from a Doolittle-type relaxation time that depends on volume/free volume.
Derive the constitutive relation with the Onsager variational principle.
Use the theory to obtain analytical yielding stress and a necking phase diagram under constant strain-rate loading.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical predictions for yielding stress and a phase diagram for neck-initiation, but does not describe experimental validation or numerical benchmarking.
watch_item: The framework is minimal and physically motivated, but its practical accuracy will depend on how well the assumed volume-dependent relaxation time captures real polymer-specific behavior.
paper_title: Shear Unfreezing Explains Yielding, Plasticity and Neck Initiation of Glassy Polymers
